text: A stone arch, half-buried in the hillside at the city's edge, marks the entrance to the old undercroft. Iron brackets once held torches; they are empty and rust-eaten now. A rope is tied to a driven spike — someone's lifeline back to the surface. You are not the first to descend, and some of those who did never returned.
Cold air rises from below, carrying the faint smell of mineral water and rot. Whatever lives down there has had a long time to settle in.
